title: Saqrlab Changes
text: Independent Codex review new/changed code. Math confirmed correct (IRT, survival, multilevel, growth, dispatcher). Five reproduced correctness bugs fixed (+16 regression tests, suite 1173 -> 1189): - R/simulate_missing.R: MAR/MNAR missingness undershot prop prop>0.5 (post-scale clamp deflated mean). Now clamp-aware: root-find sets multiplier expected missingness == prop (verified 0.9 -> 0.903, still predictor-correlated). - R/network_comparison.R: compare_centralities() crashed <2 shared states / NA centralities ((sd(NA) > 0)); guarded isTRUE(). - R/helpers_internal.R: long_to_wide() crashed zero-row input (max(integer(0))) fabricated -NA rows NA ids -> guarded; safe_bind_rows() coerced factor+character column conflicts NA -> factors normalized character first. - R/simulate_hmm.R: transition draw return NA row’s cumsum drifted 1 floating point -> last CDF bucket pinned 1. - tests/testthat/test-codex-fixes.R: regression tests five. Remaining Codex findings (input-integer/seed validation hardening, singular-Cholesky guards cor=±1, validate_recovery nested-list flattening) logged optional hardening, yet applied.

title: Saqrlab Changes
text: Full audit additive upgrade. Baseline 559 tests -> 1173 tests, 0 failures. Strict additive-contract: existing signature/return changed; simulate_data() RNG/structural output byte-identical (guarded). R CMD check (examples + R-code): 0 ERRORs new code. Safety net: - tests/testthat/test-fixture-contract.R + helper-fixture-contract.R + fixtures/fixture-contract-golden.rds: cross-package fixture tripwire. Pins exact simulate_data() output (column contract + 10-sig-digit value fingerprints) 6 types x seeds generate JStats/Carm’s ~1010 checked-JSON fixtures, RNG/structural drift fails Saqrlab’s suite first. Always-run (CRAN-skipped snapshot); negative-control verified fire drift. - tests/testthat/test-regress-{tna,compare,batch,misc}.R: characterization + reproducibility tests 45 previously-untested exported functions. Bug fixes (functions 100% broken via undefined helpers – now working + tested): - R/helpers_internal.R: implemented missing internal helpers extract_transition_matrix(), long_to_wide(), check_val_in_range(), safe_bind_rows(). Unblocks simulate_group_tna_networks/generate_group_tna_networks; compare_networks/compare_centralities/compare_edge_recovery/calculate_edge_recovery; summarize_grid_results/analyze_grid_results. - R/network_comparison.R: fixed compare_centralities tibble-coercion bug (tna::centralities() returns tibble keyed state; coerce base data.frame keyed state). New DGP modules (return saqr_sim, base R, parameter-recovery tested): - R/simulate_missing.R: inject_missingness() – MCAR/MAR/MNAR calibrated rates + metadata. - R/simulate_multilevel.R: simulate_mlm() (nesting, ICC, random slopes), simulate_growth() (latent growth curve). - R/simulate_irt.R: simulate_irt() – 1PL/2PL/3PL/GRM. - R/simulate_survival.R: simulate_survival() – Weibull/exponential/Gompertz hazards + censoring. - R/simulate_hmm.R: simulate_hmm() – hidden-Markov sequences true latent paths. New laboratory infrastructure: - R/simulate.R: simulate(type, ...) unified dispatcher + list_simulators() (16 simulators). - R/validate_recovery.R: validate_recovery(sim, estimates) – scores recovery simulated ground truth; tidy recovery_result print/summary. - R/scenarios.R: list_scenarios(), get_scenario(), run_scenario() + tidy_simulation_results(), export_simulation(). Check-cleanliness (pre-existing issues surfaced first real R CMD check): - R/Saqrlab-package.R: added @importFrom utils head tail str @importFrom stats plogis rlnorm (fixes “object ‘head’ found” namespace-load failure); registered dplyr-NSE globals. - R/simulate_latent.R: fixed buggy simulate_seq_clusters example (rows must sum 1 -> byrow=TRUE); stripped non-ASCII comments. R/ comments ASCII-clean (global_names.R name data intentionally preserved). DESCRIPTION: 0.3.0 -> 0.4.0; added Suggests lme4, mirt, survival. NAMESPACE: 73 -> 87 exports (regenerated). FEATURES.md/README.md/NEWS.md: removed stale references functions moved Nestimate; NEWS 0.4.0 entry. Tests: 1173 passing, 0 failures (559). Known pre-existing issue addressed: vignettes/tna-workflow.Rmd setup chunk runs remotes::install_github(“mohsaqr/Sonnet@…”) 404s blocks vignette build (unrelated simulation code).

title: Saqrlab Changes
text: R/simulate_latent.R: Added simulate_seq_clusters(trans_list, props, n, seq_length, init_probs, n_clusters, n_states, states, seed). Generates wide-format sequences known cluster structure. Supports explicit mode (supply K transition matrices) auto mode (random row-stochastic matrices). Returns list(data, params) T1…T{seq_length} columns + true_cluster, params containing trans_list, normalised props, per-cluster init_probs. Default n_states=10. Uses Reduce(…, accumulate=TRUE) Markov chain generation (loops). tests/testthat/test-simulate_seq_clusters.R: 24 test blocks, 31 assertions covering return structure, column names, n rows, valid cluster labels, clusters represented, valid state names, params contents, props normalisation, mixing proportions recovery large n, seed reproducibility, init_probs (shared + per-cluster + stored), auto mode (generates, 10 states, K matrices, row sums), 4 validation errors. 31 pass. man/simulate_seq_clusters.Rd: Auto-generated devtools::document().

what: 2026-03-16 — Add simulate_fa(); refactor all latent functions to list(data, params) return
title: Saqrlab Changes
text: R/simulate_latent.R: Added simulate_fa(loadings, phi, psi, n, seed). Generates MVN data Sigma = LPhiL’ + diag(psi) via Cholesky. phi validated via isSymmetric() + chol(). psi auto-computed (1 - communalities) error -factored models. sigma_implied returned params (raw eigenvalue clamp, diagonal normalisation). Refactored simulate_lpa(), simulate_lca(), simulate_regression() return list(data, params) instead data.frame + attr() — makes ground truth directly accessible JSON/CLI consumers without R-specific knowledge. tests/testthat/test-simulate_latent.R: Updated 45 existing tests use rdata/rdata / rparams. Added 30 new tests simulate_fa (structure, column names, params accuracy, sigma_implied correctness, sample covariance recovery, seed reproducibility, oblique model, user-supplied psi, single-factor, 5 validation error cases). 75 tests pass. docs/simulate_data-cookbook.md: Updated Parameter-Recovery Functions section — new return structure, added simulate_fa() full parameter table CLI JSON export example.

text: R/simulate_latent.R: New file three parameter-recovery simulation functions. simulate_lpa(means, sds, props, n, seed) generates continuous LPA data explicit profile means (n_vars × n_profiles matrix), SDs, mixing proportions. simulate_lca(item_probs, class_probs, n, seed) generates binary LCA data explicit item response probability matrix (n_items × n_classes). simulate_regression(coefs, predictor_sds, error_sd, n, seed) generates regression data lm() recovers true coefficients. three store ground truth attr(d, "true_params") validate inputs strictly. accept seed reproducibility. tests/testthat/test-simulate_latent.R: 45 TDD tests covering structure, binary/numeric columns, class label validity, true_params attributes, parameter recovery large n, seed reproducibility, input validation (dimension mismatches, --range probabilities, missing predictor_sds names, non-positive error_sd). 45 pass. docs/simulate_data-cookbook.md: Added “Parameter-Recovery Functions” section covering three new functions usage examples, parameter tables, recovery assertion patterns.

what: 2026-03-11 — Add MOGen, HONEM, HYPA higher-order network algorithms
title: Saqrlab Changes
text: R/mogen.R: New file (~400 lines). Implements Multi-Order Generative Model (Scholtes 2017; Gote & Scholtes 2023). Builds higher-order De Bruijn graphs orders k=0..K sequential trajectory data. .mogen_count_kgrams() extracts k-tuples transitions, .mogen_transition_matrix() builds row-stochastic matrices, .mogen_log_likelihood() computes hierarchical likelihood (order j step j, capping model order k), .mogen_layer_dof() counts free parameters per layer. build_mogen() selects optimal Markov order via AIC, BIC, sequential likelihood ratio tests. S3 class saqr_mogen print/summary/plot (IC comparison + likelihood plots). R/honem.R: New file (~200 lines). Implements HONEM embedding (Saebi et al. 2020). Takes HON adjacency matrix, builds row-stochastic transition matrix, computes neighborhood matrix S = (1/Z)sum(exp(-k)D^{k+1}), truncated SVD embeddings. Parameter-free — random walks skip-gram. S3 class saqr_honem print/summary/plot (2D embedding scatter). R/hypa.R: New file (~250 lines). Implements HYPA path anomaly detection (LaRock et al. 2020). Builds k-th order De Bruijn graph, fits propensity matrix Xi via iterative proportional fitting (Sinkhorn-Knopp) match observed /-strengths, computes hypergeometric CDF p-values per edge. Classifies paths /-represented user alpha. S3 class saqr_hypa print/summary/plot (expected vs observed scatter). tests/testthat/test-mogen.R: 47 tests across 8 sections. Includes order-1 recovery test (200 paths known Markov chain → BIC selects order 1) second-order detection test. tests/testthat/test-honem.R: 28 tests across 6 sections. Includes cluster separation test (within-cluster nodes closer across embedding space). tests/testthat/test-hypa.R: 32 tests across 6 sections. Includes IPF convergence test, score bounds, alpha sensitivity test. Total: 2350 tests pass (2243).

what: 2026-03-11 — Add BuildHON+ as method = “hon+” in build_hon()
title: Saqrlab Changes
text: R/hon.R: Added 7 new internal functions HON+ pipeline (~290 lines): .honp_max_divergence() (MaxDivergence upper-bound KLD pruning), .honp_build_order1() (eager order-1 StartingPoints index), .honp_extend_observation() (lazy higher-order count builder), .honp_extend_source_fast() (lazy cache lookup), .honp_add_to_rules() (HON+ variant triggers lazy extension missing prefixes), .honp_extend_rule() (recursive extension MaxDivergence pre-check), .honp_extract_rules() (top-level HON+ pipeline). Added method parameter build_hon() "hon+" default. Updated roxygen docs new parameter Saebi et al. 2020 reference. tests/testthat/test-hon.R: Extended 163 212 tests. Added Section 7 (HON+ internals, 13 tests), Section 8 (method parameter, 3 tests), Section 9 (HON vs HON+ equivalence, 3 tests), Section 10 (pyHON+ equivalence via reticulate, 4 tests exact edge/weight match). Updated Section 6 pyHON tests use method = "hon" explicitly. Performance group_regulation (2000 trajectories, 9 states): HON 0.64s vs HON+ 2.8s max_order=3. HON+ advantage parameter-free operation, raw speed scale. HON+ max_order=99 auto-discovers max order 6 2.2s.

title: Saqrlab Changes
text: R/hon.R: New file (~820 lines). Implements BuildHON algorithm (Xu, Wickramarathne & Chawla, 2016) constructing variable-order dependency networks sequential trajectory data. build_hon(data, max_order, min_freq, collapse_repeats) accepts wide data.frame list character vectors. Two-stage pipeline: (1) rule extraction via KL-divergence testing adaptive threshold order/log2(1+count), recursive order extension, source-suffix cache; (2) network wiring incoming edge rewiring higher-order nodes tail rewiring highest-order targets. Pipe-notation node naming (e.g., “B|” = B, came ). Returns saqr_hon S3 class adjacency matrix, edge list, node names, order stats. S3 methods: print, summary, plot (igraph). new dependencies. Faithful line--line translation pyHON. R/hon.R (bugfix): .hon_build_distributions() now zeros count place (matching pyHON’s BuildDistributions), .hon_kld_threshold() uses filtered totals. Previously used unfiltered counts, producing lower thresholds spurious higher-order rules. Found via group_regulation dataset testing. tests/testthat/test-hon.R: New file. 163 tests across 6 sections: input validation (8), observation counting (4), distributions (3), KL-divergence (4), end--end pipeline (10), pyHON equivalence via reticulate (5 datasets — exact edge/weight match ). pass.

what: 2026-03-06 — Add build_gimme() for GIMME (Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation)
title: Saqrlab Changes
text: R/gimme.R: New file. Implements GIMME algorithm using lavaan SEM backend. build_gimme() simple API: data, vars, id, time, ar, standardize, groupcutoff, seed. Group-level search (modification indices across subjects, prop_cutoff), pruning (remove non-significant group paths), individual-level search (Bonferroni-corrected per person) testWeights eigenvalue stability check search-prune-resume cycle. Returns saqr_gimme S3 class temporal/contemporaneous matrices (count + average), per-person standardized coefficients, psi, fit indices, group/individual paths, syntax. S3 methods: print, summary, plot (types: temporal, contemporaneous, individual, counts, fit). Uses cograph mixed-network plotting. Achieves exact equivalence gimme R package: path counts identical, standardized coefficients diff=0 across 100 randomly generated datasets (70 × 3-var, 30 × 4-var). Three key fixes: standardized betas/z-values (matching gimme’s lavInspect(fit,"std")$beta standardizedSolution()$z), resume--nonconv individual search. tests/testthat/test-gimme.R: New file. 78 tests across 10 sections: input validation, basic construction, AR paths, path counts, fit indices, reproducibility, S3 methods, gimme package equivalence (path counts identical, coefficients identical, data prep identical), 4-variable test, config storage. pass. NAMESPACE: Added export(build_gimme), S3 methods saqr_gimme. DESCRIPTION: Added lavaan Suggests.

title: Saqrlab Changes
text: R/boot_glasso.R: New file (~650 lines). Exported boot_glasso(x, iter, cs_iter, cs_drop, alpha, gamma, nlambda, centrality, cor_method, ncores, seed) — single-call bootstrap EBICglasso partial correlation networks. Combines nonparametric edge/centrality CIs, case-dropping CS-coefficient, edge inclusion probabilities, thresholded network, edge/centrality difference tests, predictability CIs. Accepts data frame, matrix, glasso netobject. Ten internal helpers. S3 class boot_glasso print, summary(type=), plot(type=) methods. 6 plot types: edges, stability, edge_diff, centrality_diff, inclusion, network. Reuses existing GLASSO pipeline. Supports parallel via mclapply. new dependencies. R/boot_glasso.R (bootnet equivalence): Rewrote .bg_case_drop() match bootnet’s random-per-iteration approach (iteration randomly picks ONE drop proportion, fixed iterations per proportion). Changed .bg_cs_coefficient() use mean(cors > 0.7) > 0.95 (matching bootnet’s proportion--threshold, quantile-based). Changed case-dropping correlation Spearman Pearson match bootnet’s cor0(). tests/testthat/test-boot_glasso.R: 228 tests total. Original 217 tests plus 11 new bootnet equivalence tests: original network matches bootnet across 5 seeds (max diff < 0.01), edge CIs r > 0.99, strength CIs r > 0.99, CS-coefficient matches corStability within 0.15, strength matches qgraph::centrality()$InDegree. what: 2026-02-23 — Snapshot-based temporal network metrics (with tsna parity)
title: Saqrlab Changes
text: R/temporal_network.R: Added .compute_snapshot_metrics() — computes 21 igraph metrics (13 graph-level, 8 node-level) across time bin snapshots single pass. Graph-level: density_bins, reciprocity, mutuality, dyad_census, transitivity, centralization (degree/betweenness/closeness), n_components, triad_census, mean_distance, diameter, assortativity. Node-level: closeness_snapshot, betweenness_snapshot, eigenvector, page_rank, hub_score, authority_score, constraint, coreness. R/temporal_network.R: Changed .build_static_snapshots() point--time edge inclusion (onset <= bin_start & terminus > bin_start) + igraph::simplify() multi-edge collapse, matching tsna’s network.collapse(). R/temporal_network.R: Directed transitivity uses matrix formula sum(² * ) / sum(²) (directed weak transitivity) matching sna::gtrans(measure="weak"). Vacuous case (two-paths) returns 1 matching sna convention. Undirected uses igraph::transitivity(type="global") NaN→1. R/temporal_network.R: Updated print (mean transitivity + reciprocity), summary (graph-level time series section), plot (4 new types: reciprocity, centralization, eigenvector, dyad_census). tests/testthat/test-temporal_network.R: Added section 17 32 tests — dimensions (5), known values (10), range/validity (5), tsna equivalence via .compare_snapshot_metrics() helper across 6 datasets: chain, star, dense directed, large 30v/100e directed, large 20v/60e undirected, 50v/200e directed (6), plot types (5), additional (1). Exact numerical match density, reciprocity, mutuality, triad census, transitivity. Total: 432 temporal_network tests, 1262 full suite, pass, 0 warnings.

what: Two simulation tiers (the central design split)
title: CLAUDE.md
text: package deliberately maintains two incompatible simulation interfaces — know one function belongs touching : Explicit-parameter tier → returns saqr_sim object. pass ground truth (simulate_ttest(mean_a = 0, mean_b = 0.5, ...)) recover $params. parameter-recovery testing. Covers simulate_statistical.R, simulate_latent.R, simulate_longitudinal.R. Random-parameter tier → simulate_data() returns bare data.frame (params attributes). seed generates random structure stress/robustness testing. wrapped saqr_sim — wrapping breaks ~169 existing tests, two tiers stay separate design.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Explicit-parameter statistical simulation — saqr_sim ground-truth $params: - simulate_statistical.R — simulate_ttest() (params include cohens_d), simulate_anova() (eta_squared), simulate_correlation(), simulate_clusters(), simulate_prediction() (r_squared). - simulate_longitudinal.R — simulate_longitudinal(): VAR(1) panel data mlVAR/ESM explicit temporal (B), contemporaneous, matrices; ESM day/beep structure; auto-generated B rescaled guarantee stationarity. - simulate_latent.R — simulate_lpa(), simulate_lca(), simulate_regression(), simulate_fa(), simulate_seq_clusters(). TNA-specific simulation — generates fitted tna model objects directly: - simulate_tna_network.R — single fitted TNA model learning states - simulate_tna_networks.R — multiple TNA models, group networks, HTNA/MLNA matrices (simulate_tna_networks(), simulate_group_tna_networks(), simulate_htna(), simulate_mlna(), simulate_mtna()) - simulate_sequences.R — Markov chain sequences transition matrices (simulate_sequences()) - simulate_sequences_advanced.R — sequences stability/instability patterns - simulate_matrix.R — raw transition matrices (simulate_matrix(), simulate_tna_matrix()) Random-parameter simulation — bare data.frame, params attributes: - simulate_data.R — 15 dataset types: ttest, anova, correlation, clusters, factor_analysis, prediction, mlvar, probit, meta, count, reliability, gam, nma, power, cthmm. Supports complexity parameter edge-case injection (NA, outliers, ties, etc.) batch generation. Network/graph simulation: - simulate_edge_list.R, simulate_igraph.R, simulate_network.R — social network structures - simulate_long_data.R — hierarchical long-format group data - simulate_onehot_data.R — one-hot encoded sequences

Invisible zero tests. Use codetools::checkUsagePackage(=TRUE) enumerate undefined-symbol references instead discovering one cascade time. [tna::centralities returns tibble]: class tna_centralities,tbl_df,... state COLUMN positional rownames – cent[, measure] stays 1-col tibble (list) sd()/cor() choke (“‘list’ object coerced type ‘double’”). Coerce .data.frame() key rownames state extracting measure columns. [head/tail/str need importFrom utils]: defining S3 methods head.saqr_sim/tail.saqr_sim/str.saqr_sim @export adds S3method(...) generic import. Without @importFrom utils head tail str namespace fails load R CMD check (“object ‘head’ found whilst loading namespace”) – works devtools::load_all() utils attached. dim/names/print/summary/.data.frame base generics need import. [non-ASCII: strings ok, comments warn]: Encoding: UTF-8, non-ASCII STRING LITERALS permitted (e.g. accented names global_names.R) non-ASCII COMMENTS/code triggers R CMD check “non-ASCII characters” WARNING. Em-dash/ellipsis/arrows roxygen usual culprits. [agents: verify code claims acting]: parallel audit agents flagged two false-positive “bugs” – GROUP_REGULATION_ACTIONS “undefined” (defined simulate_long_data.R) paste0("S", 1:n_states) producing “S1.0” (R’s 1:5 always integer, ’s “S1”). Always confirm flagged bug running package “fixing” working code.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [saqr_sim backward compat]: Adding S3 class list(data, params) via class(obj) <- c("saqr_sim", "list") fully backward compatible — $data, $params, names() keep working. [.saqr_sim method delegates $data subscripting works . [saqr_sim vs data.frame returns]: wrap simulate_data() saqr_sim — 169 existing tests treat result bare data.frame (d$group, names(d), .data.frame(d)). $ list goes list elements, data columns. need $.saqr_sim fall-$data columns, creates name collision risk fields like “type”. Two tiers right design: saqr_sim explicit-parameter functions, bare data.frame random-parameter simulate_data(). [nrow/ncol S3 generics]: base R, nrow() ncol() call NROW()/NCOL() use dim(). Defining dim.saqr_sim() sufficient — explicit nrow.saqr_sim ncol.saqr_sim get exported regular functions (methods) pollute NAMESPACE. Just define dim. [population eta-squared]: True population eta-squared = ss_between / (ss_between + ss_within) ss_between = sum(n_k * (mu_k - grand_mean)^2) ss_within = sum((n_k - 1) * sigma_k^2). parameter, sample estimate. [population R-squared]: True R² = var(signal) / (var(signal) + error_sd^2). Computed actual linear combination variance generated data, population formula. Accounts continuous categorical predictor contributions. [pooled SD Cohen’s d]: Use sqrt(((n_a-1)*sd_a^2 + (n_b-1)*sd_b^2) / (n_a+n_b-2)) pooled SD Cohen’s d. standard pooled estimator, simple average variances. [VAR stationarity]: VAR(1) process y(t) = mu + B(y(t-1) - mu) + e stationary iff eigenvalues B modulus < 1. Rescaling B <- B * (0.95 / max(Mod(eigen(B)$values))) guarantees preserving relative coefficient structure.  [.ensure_pd vs .nearest_pd]: Two PD-fixing helpers now exist: .ensure_pd() simulate_longitudinal.R (eigenvalue clamp ) .nearest_pd() simulate_data.R (eigenvalue clamp + correlation normalisation). latter normalises correlation matrix; former preserves scale covariance matrices.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [package split cleanup]: splitting packages, check (1) functions called remaining code moved, (2) test files moved functions, (3) unused DESCRIPTION Imports. three bit us Saqrlab/Nestimate split.  [package structural review]: Run grep -rn "function_name" R/ across packages verify every called function defined somewhere. Don’t rely git history memory.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [matrix() list input]: matrix(list_of_vectors, nrow=..., byrow=TRUE) creates list-matrix (cell holds vector), numeric matrix. Use matrix(unlist(list_of_vectors), nrow=..., byrow=TRUE) get numeric matrix. rowSums() error list-matrix “‘x’ must numeric”. [Reduce accumulate Markov chains]: Reduce(f, x=seq_len(T-1), accumulate=TRUE, init=s0) generates T states (init + T-1 ), perfect Markov chain simulation without loops. Returns list; use unlist() states scalar strings.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [tna input formats]: tna::tna() accepts data frame sequences (rows=sequences, cols=time points) square numeric matrix (transition frequencies). Column prefix can “T” (group_regulation) just “V”. [group_regulation_long]: Structure Actor (int), Achiever (chr), Group (num), Course (chr), Time (POSIXct), Action (chr). 27,533 rows, 9 unique actions (adapt, cohesion, consensus, coregulate, discuss, emotion, monitor, plan, synthesis). [group_regulation]: Wide format T1-T26 columns (V1-V26). 2000 rows. NAs trailing (variable-length sequences). [tna model structure]: tna object list weights (transition matrix), inits (initial probs), labels (state names), data (encoded sequences). attr(model, "type") “relative” probability weights. [transition counting]: build frequency matrix sequences, count consecutive pairs (action[t], action[t+1]) within sequence. long data, split ID order time first. wide data, iterate across columns per row. Use table() factors vectorized counting. [frequencies.R]: File contains two functions: frequencies() builds transition frequency matrix (state x state), convert_sequence_format() converts frequency counts, onehot, edgelist, follows format. accept wide long input.  [pivot_longer default]: tidyr::pivot_longer() creates “name” column default names_to specified. column harmless used downstream operations. [R CMD check]: Package pre-existing warning \usage entries man pages (new code). Hidden files (.claude, .github) non-portable file paths docs/ cache trigger NOTEs.  [glasso]: glasso::glasso() returns list w (estimated covariance), wi (estimated precision/inverse covariance). Warm starts use start = "warm" w.init wi.init. Cold start default. [EBICglasso validation]: native EBIC + glasso implementation matches qgraph::EBICglasso exactly test data (partial correlations, edges, sparsity). [compositional frequency data]: Per-sequence action frequencies compositional (rows sum ~constant sequence length). causes partial correlations strongly negative. Expected behavior, bug. [glasso vs glassoFast]: zero deps use Fortran. glassoFast ~2x faster available WebR (WASM) binary. glasso WebR repo. glassoFast also lacks penalize.diagonal param (always penalizes diagonal; workaround: pass rho matrix zero diagonal). Chose glasso WebR compatibility. [engagement stslist]: tna::engagement stslist object. Converting frequency via convert_sequence_format(.data.frame(engagement), format = "frequency") produces % column (void/padding marker). Non-syntactic column names like % need auto-cleaning. [cograph::splot]: Preferred tplot() richer rendering. Supports pie_values/pie_colors predictability pies, donut_values/donut_color donuts, node_fill node colors, edge_labels = TRUE edge weights, title param directly (unlike tplot). Use .call(cograph::splot, dots) pass args programmatically. Default layout "oval". [cograph::tplot]: Thin qgraph-compatible wrapper around splot. Uses pie/pieColor (qgraph names). accept main — use title param title() call. [build_network]: Renamed pcor_network() build_network() method parameter. Supports "glasso" (aliases: "ebicglasso", "regularized"), "pcor" (alias: "partial"), "cor" (alias: "correlation"). S3 class "psych_network". Main result field $network_matrix ($pcor_matrix). Auto-cleans non-syntactic column names, -NA columns, NA rows, zero-variance columns. Uses glasso package (WebR-compatible) glasso method. [build_network multilevel]: level parameter ("", "within", "") decomposes data estimation. = aggregate person means; Within = person-mean center variable (drops persons <2 obs). level = "" returns psych_network_ml S3 class. id_col required level set. Must data frame input (matrix supported). id_vals alignment cleaned rows uses row_mask boolean vector.   [predictability]: Node predictability (R²) computed 1 - 1/Omega_jj glasso/pcor (precision matrix). cor method, uses multiple R² correlation matrix subsets (crossprod(r, solve(R_nn, r))). Follows Haslbeck & Waldorp (2018). Validated mgm: r=0.999, mean |diff|=0.008. Zero dependencies added. Shown plots via pie_values splot, printed default. [plot styling]: Use cograph::splot() pie_values predictability, node_fill pastel colors, edge_labels = TRUE, theme = "colorblind", node_size = 8. Hand-picked palette 15 distinct pastels cycles via rep_len(). multilevel, use par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) .exit() restore. [group_regulation_long nesting]: Actor appears exactly 1 Group (2000 actors, 200 groups, 10 actors/group). multilevel decomposition, use Group grouping variable (id_col = c("Group", "Actor") — first element grouping variable).

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [HON+ lazy observation]: BuildHON+ (Saebi et al. 2020) builds order-1 counts eagerly, extends lazily via StartingPoints index. source key maps list c(traj_index, position) pairs. Extension looks one step back trajectory build higher-order counts demand. avoids building unused high-order observations.   [HON+ R performance]: HON+ slower HON R moderate scale (2.8s vs 0.6s 2000 trajectories, max_order=3) due higher per-operation overhead environment-based hash maps list appending StartingPoints. advantage parameter-free operation (max_order tuning needed). max_order=99 (parameter-free mode), HON+ auto-discovers right order (6) 2.2s. [reticulate testthat]: Three reticulate quirks test_file context: (1) py$x <- val fails — use py_set_attr(py, "x", val), (2) Python list elements don’t auto-convert — use py_to_r() first, (3) Python tuples become R environments — return dicts instead reliable conversion. [HON+ equivalence threshold]: HON+ uses strict > comparison KLD vs threshold (matching pyHON+). exactly 3 observations, KLD can equal threshold exactly (e.g., = 1.0), extension doesn’t trigger. Need 4+ observations push threshold KLD. [MOGen De Bruijn k-grams]: order k, nodes k-tuples encoded .HON_SEP. Edges connect overlapping k-tuples extracted (k+1)-length sub-paths. Use \x02 edge pair separator (different \x01 used k-gram encoding). table() paste’d keys efficient counting approach. [MOGen hierarchical likelihood]: order-k model, step j uses order min(j-1, k). Step 1 = order 0 (marginal), step 2 = order 1, …, step k+1 onward = order k. Cumulative DOF = sum per-layer DOFs. makes nested model comparison valid LRT.  [HONEM random walk]: HONEM (Saebi et al. 2020) use random walks skip-gram/Word2Vec. ’s direct matrix factorization: neighborhood matrix S = (1/Z)sum(exp(-k)D^{k+1}) truncated SVD. Parameter-free. [HYPA IPF convergence]: Iterative proportional fitting (Sinkhorn-Knopp) HYPA’s Xi matrix converges quickly (~10-20 iterations tol=1e-6). Must respect edge structure: Xi[,j]=0 wherever adj[,j]=0. Row/col scaling preserves sparsity pattern. [HYPA hypergeometric]: phyper(k, K, N-K, n) computes CDF P(X<=k). Must round() Xi values integers hypergeometric parameters. Clamping K [0, N] prevents invalid parameters.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [Bayesian transition networks]: viable without informative prior network. Uniform Dirichlet prior just Laplace smoothing, making transitions non-zero → bootstrap flags everything significant. useful researcher theoretical/prior transition matrix update data. markovchain::markovchainFit(method = "map") supports Bayesian estimation uniform priors. [markovchain package]: Installed. markovchainFit() supports method = "mle", "laplace" (add-1 smoothing), "map" (Bayesian). MAP returns $estimate, $expectedValue, $standardError, $confidenceInterval, $logLikelihood. supports uniform priors — informative prior input. [existing comparison functions]: TNA-specific. compare_networks() compares two TNA models (correlation, RMSE, edge weights). compare_network_estimation() compares TNA model types (tna/atna/ctna/ftna) via sampling stability. compare_edge_recovery() binary edge TP/FP/FN. cross_validate_tna() cross-validates TNA model types. None work psych_network objects build_network(). [estimate_network]: Universal estimation engine. estimate_network(data, method, params, scaling, threshold, level, id_col) → saqr_network S3 class. Supports 6 built-methods: relative, frequency, co_occurrence (transition); cor, pcor, glasso (association). params list composability key — stores method-specific args replay bootstrap/grid search. build_network() now delegates estimate_network() internally converts output psych_network backward compat via .saqr_to_psych_network().  [tabulate byrow]: using pair_idx = (from_int - 1) * n_states + to_int tabulate(), resulting flat vector row-major order. R’s matrix() fills column-major default, byrow = TRUE required get correct mat[, ] indexing. Without , /get silently swapped.  [count_transitions NA handling]: counting transitions, NAs must stripped creating pairs. Instead, create consecutive pairs first (= actions[-n], = actions[-1]), filter pairs either side NA. Stripping NAs first creates false transitions bridging gaps (e.g. , NA, B → falsely create →B). [frequencies vectorized]: frequencies() now delegates .count_transitions() uses vectorized base R (matrix slicing + tabulate) wide format data.table long format. significantly faster old per-row lapply approach. [bootstrap_network]: Universal bootstrap estimator estimate_network(). Two paths: transition methods use fast pre-computed per-sequence counts (single tabulate + colSums per iteration); association methods call full estimator per iteration. Returns saqr_bootstrap S3 class CIs, p-values, mean/SD, significant edges, pruned network. ~2.8x faster tna::bootstrap() 2000 sequences / 500 iterations due colSums 2D matrix (C-level) vs apply 3D array (R-level). [bootstrap precomputation]: Per-sequence count matrices built n_seq x (n_states^2) 2D matrices using combined flat index (row - 1) * nbins + pair_idx single tabulate(). bootstrap iteration needs colSums(trans_2d[sampled_rows, ]) + lightweight post-processing (row-normalize relative).  [bootstrap summary]: Long-format data frame built via data.table::data.table() speed. Directed networks filter weight != 0 & != ; undirected filter weight != 0 & < (upper triangle ). [bootstrap refactor]: Dropped long-format precompute helpers (bootstrap now requires wide format, errors message use convert_sequence_format()). Merged transition+co-occurrence wide precompute single .precompute_per_sequence_wide(). Inlined .postprocess_transition() vapply loop — builds matrix without dimnames since ’s flattened immediately. Extracted .select_state_cols() shared helper estimators.R. File went 787 → 598 lines (~24% reduction). 566 tests pass. [build_network consolidation]: build_network() now single entry point 6 estimation methods (split build_network() association estimate_network() ). Returns netobject class (psych_network/saqr_network). estimate_network() now deprecated wrapper calls build_network(). method parameter required (default). Shared helpers (.resolve_method_alias(), .apply_scaling(), .extract_edges_from_matrix(), .decompose_multilevel()) live estimate_network.R. S3 methods (print, plot, predictability) live build_network.R.   [testthat edition 3 deprecation]: .Deprecated() emits warning, testthat edition 3 (set DESCRIPTION Config/testthat/edition: 3) treats warnings test failures. calls deprecated functions tests must wrapped suppressWarnings(). [plot predictability conditional]: plot.netobject adds predictability pies association methods (method %% c("glasso", "pcor", "cor")), transition methods. avoids errors since transition methods don’t produce precision matrices. [netobject $data field]: `netobject$datastores **cleaned** data estimator, raw input. Association methods: cleaned numeric matrix (NAs, non-numeric, zero-variance dropped .prepare_association_input()). Transition methods: original data.frame -. Matrix input (e.g. pre-computed correlation): NULL (row-level data). $data` field added `netobject_ml` directly — sub-network stores . Downstream code (`permutation_test`, `bootstrap_network`) can use `$data` directly without re-cleaning. [permutation_test]: Universal permutation test network comparison. Works 6 methods. Transition methods use fast precomputed counts (bootstrap). Association methods use optimized direct calls (see ). P-values: (sum(|perm_diff| >= |obs_diff|) + 1) / (iter + 1). Effect size: obs_diff / sd(perm_diffs). Running accumulators avoid storing full 3D array. Supports paired mode (within-pair swaps) p.adjust correction. S3 class: saqr_permutation. [permutation_test optimization]: Association path optimized speed: (1) pre-clean pooled numeric matrix loop, (2) direct cor()/solve() calls cor/pcor (estimator dispatch), (3) glasso::glassopath() 50 lambda values + .select_ebic_from_path() glasso. glassopath() 14.5x faster separate glasso() calls ’s single Fortran call full regularization path. Lambda path uses .compute_lambda_path() pooled correlation matrix. [permutation_test vs NCT]: 100-dataset × 1000-iteration benchmark NetworkComparisonTest::NCT: cor p-values identical (r=1.0, 100% agreement, 1.9x faster); glasso p-value r=0.9999, 99.9% agreement, 2.6x faster. Previous 98.6% cor disagreement caused RNG stream divergence (see sampling fix ). [sample.int RNG divergence]: sample.int(n) (full permutation) consumes n RNG values; sample.int(n, k) consumes k. NCT uses partial draw sample(1:n, k). Using sample.int(n) taking first k causes RNG state diverge NCT iteration 1, producing different permutations. Fix: use sample.int(n_total, n_x) match NCT’s RNG consumption exactly. seed → identical results. [permutation_test vs tna]: 100-dataset simulation tna::permutation_test: identical p-values (r=1.0) negligible effect size differences TNA (relative) CNA (co_occurrence) methods. Confirms numerical correctness. [glassopath]: glasso::glassopath(s, rholist, trace = 0, penalize.diagonal) returns $wi 3D array (p × p × n_lambda). Use .select_ebic_from_path() pick best precision matrix via EBIC. Much faster calling glasso() separately lambda warm-starts previous solution internally. [fixed lambda rejected]: Using fixed regularization lambda (original network) across permutations drops significance agreement 99% 93%. Must re-select lambda via EBIC permutation valid inference. glassopath() approach preserves proper model selection fast.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [GLLA factorial scaling]: GLLA Vandermonde basis t^k gives Taylor coefficients, derivatives. Row k weight matrix must multiplied k! get actual k-th derivative. Without , velocity (k=1) correct (1!=1), acceleration (k=2) factor 2. [.count_transitions_long time=NULL]: Passing time = NULL .count_transitions_long() causes error NULL %% names(dt) returns logical(0) can’t used (). calling within .matrices_from_raw_data() (data already split time), pass actual time_col name instead NULL. [velocity_tna]: Implemented velocity_tna() R/velocity_tna.R. Supports three methods: "regression" (default, OLS beta), "glla", "finite_difference". Three input formats: list matrices, cograph::tna_windows() output, raw data frame. Returns tna_velocity S3 class print/summary/plot methods. Plot supports “network” (cograph::splot green/red edges), “series” (matplot regression fit lines), “heatmap” (image diverging palette). Regression returns edge_stats slope, SE, t-value, p-value, R² per edge. [.vector column-major]: .vector(matrix) R flattens column-major. iterating flattened edge indices j=1..n², correct mapping row_idx = ((j-1) %% n) + 1, col_idx = ((j-1) %/% n) + 1. Swapping %% %/% transposes result silently — subtle bug passes dimension checks puts values wrong cells. [Ising model estimator]: Implemented .estimator_ising() R/estimators.R. Uses nodewise L1-penalized logistic regression (glmnet) EBIC model selection (IsingFit algorithm, van Borkulo et al. 2014). Parameters: gamma (EBIC, default 0.25), nlambda (default 100), rule (“”/“” symmetrization). Requires glmnet (Suggests). Method name: "ising", alias: "isingfit". Returns undirected weighted adjacency + thresholds. Validated: exact numerical match IsingFit::IsingFit across 50+ random configurations. [Ising EBIC formula]: IsingFit uses nodewise EBIC: -2*loglik + k*log(n) + 2*gamma*k*log(p-1) p-1 number predictors per regression. differs graphical EBIC (used EBICglasso) uses 4*gamma*k*log(p). Using wrong formula selects different lambdas ~20% cases. IsingFit’s min_sum defaults -Inf (row filtering). [Ising symmetrization]: IsingFit’s rule uses simple (W + t(W)) / 2 — averaging values including zeros. “average nonzero values ”. rule masks (W!=0) & (t(W)!=0) first, averages.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [temporal BFS convergence]: Temporal BFS (earliest-arrival) can require multiple passes edges strict temporal DAG order (.e., earlier-onset edges can update arrivals unlock later-onset edges already scanned). Using changed flag loop bounded n_vertices iterations guarantees convergence. pure temporal DAGs, single pass suffices. [backward temporal reachability]: Implemented reversing edges negating times, running forward BFS. target vertex gets start_time = -Inf, .finite() returns FALSE — target already excluded “reachable” count. subtract 1 . [sd() single value]: sd() length-1 vector returns NA, 0. computing burstiness coefficient B = (sigma - mu) / (sigma + mu), must handle length(iet) < 2 setting sigma = 0 avoid NA propagation. [temporal density normalization]: density = sum(durations) / (n*(n-1) * time_span) directed networks. single edge scales proportionally time_span (longer edge = longer span), density stays constant. test density differences, vary number edges add parallel edges within time span. [temporal_network]: Implemented R/temporal_network.R. Input: edge list (, , onset, terminus). Core algorithms: temporal BFS (earliest-arrival), time-varying degree, forward/backward reachability, temporal closeness, temporal betweenness, formation/dissolution, edge durations, IET + burstiness, temporal density, static snapshots. new dependencies (igraph + ggplot2 already Imports). Returns temporal_network S3 class print/summary/plot methods. 192 tests (including 20 tsna equivalence tests).  [tsna tDegree point--time]: tsna::tDegree() computes degree time point instant (onset <= t & terminus > t), interval overlap (onset < bin_end & terminus > bin_start). integer-aligned times, agree. floating-point times, differ — edges starting mid-bin counted interval overlap point--time snapshot. Use onset <= bin_start & terminus > bin_start match tsna. [tsna tEdgeFormation uses exact equality]: tsna::tEdgeFormation() counts edges onset == t (exact equality). matches interval-based count (onset >= bin_start & onset < bin_end) onset times grid-aligned (integer). floating-point times, tsna misses edges since onset == grid_point rarely true. interval approach general; compare tsna integer-time datasets. [tsna tEdgeDissolution convention]: tsna records dissolution terminus time (first moment edge gone). Uses [bin_start, bin_end) half-open interval, formation. original (bin_start, bin_end] convention shifted one bin. [networkDynamic merges overlapping spells]: two edge spells (tail, head) pair overlap time, networkDynamic merges single activity period. changes total edge duration (union vs sum). implementation sums durations per pair without merging overlaps. Compare edge durations tsna datasets without overlapping spells pair. [numeric vertex sorting]: vertex names numeric strings (“1”, “2”, …, “10”), sort() gives alphabetical order (“1”, “10”, “2”, …). Must detect numeric names sort .numeric() match tsna’s integer vertex indexing. [undirected temporal BFS]: Temporal BFS traverses →default. undirected networks, must add reverse edges (→onset/terminus) running BFS, otherwise reachability asymmetric. .compute_all_temporal_paths() temporal_paths() need fix.    [igraph empty graph metrics]: empty graphs (0 edges): reciprocity() → NaN, transitivity(type="global") → NaN, centr_clo()$centralization → NaN, mean_distance(unconnected=TRUE) → NaN, assortativity_degree() → NaN, constraint() → NaN vertices. page_rank() → uniform 1/n. hub_score()/hits_scores()$hub → 1. coreness() → 0. centr_degree() → 0. centr_betw() → 0.  [sna vacuous transitivity]: sna::gtrans() returns 1 two-paths (vacuously true). igraph::transitivity() returns NaN. tsna parity, use 1 vacuous case. [snapshot point--time parity]: tsna’s tSnaStats() uses network.collapse() snapshots edges active specific time point: onset <= t & terminus > t. original interval-overlap approach (onset < bin_end & terminus > bin_start) included edges yet active bin_start starting within bin. Changed point--time + igraph::simplify() exact tsna match.   [centr_degree directed star]: directed -star (→B,→C,→D,→E), centr_degree(g) default mode=“” (total degree) gives centralization ~0.375 (near 1). -degree centralization via centr_degree(g, mode="") gives 0.8. Default mode averages +degrees dilutes star pattern.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [simulate_data cholesky]: generating multivariate data correlation matrix, use chol(R) (upper Cholesky) Z %*% L Z iid standard normal. .nearest_pd() helper (eigenvalue clamping + unit diagonal normalization) ensures matrix always valid even arbitrary -diagonal assignment. [simulate_data factor analysis n constraint]: factanal() requires n > p (observations > variables). generator enforces n >= max(p + 10, 50) avoid convergence issues. 2–4 factors × 3–4 items = 6–16 variables, minimum n 50. [simulate_data seed-driven variation]: Using sample() runif() start generator pick structural parameters (n, k, d, effect sizes) ranges makes different seeds produce structurally different datasets. seed also governs data , reproducibility exact.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [boot_glasso CS-coefficient]: CS-coefficient matching bootnet requires three key details: (1) case-drop iteration randomly picks ONE drop proportion cs_drop levels (bootnet’s random-per-iteration approach, fixed iterations per proportion). (2) Uses Pearson correlation original centralities, Spearman — bootnet’s cor0() Pearson. (3) CS = max(drop_prop) mean(cors > 0.7) > 0.95 (proportion--threshold, 5th-percentile-based). three fixes, CS matches bootnet::corStability() within 0.15 100% seeds tested (mean diff 0.006). [boot_glasso input dispatch]: .prepare_association_input() treats raw matrices (n x p) correlation matrices (checks nrow == ncol). Must convert data.frame first passing raw observation data matrix.  [boot_glasso edge diff scalability]: Pairwise edge difference test O(n_edges^2 * iter). p=30 (435 edges), ’s 94,830 pairs. function returns NULL n_edges > 500 avoid excessive computation. large networks, use centrality difference tests instead. [mlvar within-centering]: Person-mean centering Y (outcome) X (predictor) panel VAR absorbs random intercept, making OLS equivalent fixed-effects estimator (lmer(..., (1|id))). Must center sides — centering Y leaves intercept X. [mlvar df correction]: within-centering, effective df = n_obs - d - n_subjects (n_obs - d). n_subjects penalty accounts absorbed person-specific intercepts. SE_correct = SE_ols * sqrt(df_ols / df_correct). [glasso symmetry]: glasso::glasso() precision matrix ($wi) can floating-point asymmetry ~1e-6. .precision_to_pcor() propagates . Must force symmetry (pcor + t(pcor)) / 2 conversion. [mlvar VAR simulation]: simulate VAR(1) known B: y_t = mu + B %*% (y_{t-1} - mu) + noise. Person-specific mu creates -subject variation. Innovation noise correlated Cholesky creates contemporaneous structure. Diagonal B controls autoregression (0.2–0.5 range works well ESM data).  [mlvar vs mlVAR equivalence]: mlvar(standardize=FALSE) produces identical temporal coefficients mlVAR::mlVAR(estimator="lmer", temporal="fixed", scale=FALSE) — max diff ~1e-15 across 25 seeds. P-values differ <0.002 (t-distribution vs mlVAR’s normal approximation). Residual correlations match r>0.9999 (lmer REML vs OLS). Person mean correlations match r>0.999. Significance agreement >95% alpha=0.05. [mlVAR estimator=“lm”]: "lm" estimator requires temporal="unique" (option). uses LSDV (person dummies) instead centering — algebraically equivalent gives different scaling coefficients due -person means included. "lmer" estimator temporal="fixed" closer centering approach. [mlVAR contemporaneous]: mlVAR uses unregularized nodewise regression residuals + matrix inversion partial correlations (GLASSO). GLASSO-based approach produces sparser networks. underlying residual correlation matrices match, final partial correlations differ method design. [mlVAR p-value method]: mlVAR uses 2*(1-pnorm(|beta/se|)) (normal approximation). implementation uses 2*pt(|t|, df, lower.tail=FALSE) (exact t-distribution). 1000+ observations, difference <0.002.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [proximity timeline]: temporal_network() uses time_interval parameter (n_bins) control temporal resolution. number bins derived ceiling((t_max - t_min) / time_interval). Pre-computed metric matrices (eigenvector, page_rank, etc.) n_vertices × n_bins vertex_names rownames. [cmdscale disconnected graphs]: igraph::distances() returns Inf unreachable vertex pairs. Must replace Inf large finite value (e.g. n_vertices) passing stats::cmdscale(), otherwise MDS fails. Wrap tryCatch() degenerate cases (e.g. zero-edge snapshots). [ggplot2 legend auto-hide]: Setting theme(legend.position = "none") hides legend. proximity timeline, auto-hide n_vertices > 15 vertex_group provided, since individual vertex colors become unreadable.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [cograph::mcml naming conflict]: cograph::mcml() deprecated alias cluster_summary(). Saqrlab’s MCML function must named differently (build_mcml) avoid masking packages loaded. library(cograph) masks Saqrlab’s export silently. [build_network matrix input]: build_network() method = "relative" accept pre-computed matrix — relative estimator calls .count_transitions() requires data.frame. pre-computed matrices, pass directly cograph::cluster_summary() instead. [cluster_summary type=“tna”]: cograph::cluster_summary(..., type = "tna") row-normalizes -cluster matrix (diagonal = 0) within-cluster matrix. $$within elements tna-class objects $weights, $inits, $labels, $data. [cograph as_cograph edge list]: cograph::as_cograph(df, directed=TRUE) accepts edge list data.frame //weight columns. cograph::to_matrix(g) converts square matrix. Nodes g$nodes label name columns. Custom columns (like cluster) can added g$nodes downstream use. [mcml recode vs cluster_summary]: cograph::cluster_summary() aggregates node-level matrix post-hoc (zeroes diagonal, averages blocks, renormalizes). recode/filter approach estimates directly sequences: recode states cluster labels , filter non-member states NA within. produce fundamentally different results — recode approach preserves actual transition patterns including self-loops correct normalization. Always use recode/filter raw sequences available. [bootstrap_mcml recode]: bootstrap -cluster network, recode states cluster labels wide data, call bootstrap_network() recoded data. within-cluster, filter data non-cluster states become NA (bootstrap_network handles NAs treating sequence breaks). [mcml TNA alignment]: -cluster network includes self-loops (non-zero diagonal), matching TNA counts consecutive transitions. collapse consecutive -cluster states — user explicitly rejected approach. matrix standard TNA model recoded data.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [gimme algorithm]: GIMME (Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation) uses uSEM: y(t) = Ay(t) + (t-1) + e(t). = contemporaneous effects (DAG), B = lagged effects. Path search via lavaan modification indices (MI). Group paths: MI significant >75% converging subjects. Individual paths: Bonferroni-corrected MI per person. Pruning: remove group paths non-significant z-values across >50% subjects. [gimme lavaan syntax]: Base syntax needs explicit variance (~~), intercept (~1), exogenous covariance terms lagged variables. Without exogenous covariances, lavaan treats lagged vars zero correlation, breaking MI calculation. AR paths (V1 ~ V1lag) can optional via ar parameter. [gimme vs gimme package]: Exact equivalence — path counts identical, standardized coefficients diff=0 across 100 randomly generated datasets (70 × 3-var, 30 × 4-var, random /Phi/Psi, 8-15 subjects, 80-150 obs). Three fixes needed: (1) standardized betas via lavInspect(fit, "std")$beta (parameterEstimates()$est), (2) standardized z-values via standardizedSolution()$z pruning (parameterEstimates()$z), (3) resume forward search nonconv/stability removal, just z-pruning.  [gimme MI extraction]: lavaan::modindices() returns data.frame lhs, op, rhs, mi columns. Filter op == "~" (regression paths ). Match candidate paths lhs_rhs concatenation. MI > 0 model converges = eligible selection. [gimme testWeights]: Stability check standardized beta matrix. Extracts lavInspect(fit, "std")$beta, subsets endo rows × coln columns (= c(lag_names, endo_names)). Tests eigenvalues lagged block (cols 1:p) contemporaneous block (cols p+1:2p). Re(eigenvalue) >= 1 → unstable. individual search, unstable models trigger path removal (pop last added) stable, z-prune, optionally resume forward search excluding removed paths. Without , coefficients can differ ~0.05 gimme. [gimme standardized output]: gimme stores standardized betas (lavInspect(fit, "std")$beta) path_est_mats, rounded 4 digits. Using unstandardized parameterEstimates()$est produces different values. Must use standardized exact match. [gimme standardized z-values]: gimme uses standardizedSolution()$z z-pruning individual search (via return.zs), parameterEstimates()$z. standardized z can differ substantially unstandardized (e.g. 2.90 vs 2.61), flipping prune decisions near Bonferroni cutoff.

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [BuildHON tuple keys R]: Python uses tuples dict keys. R hashable vector type. Solution: encode character vectors paste(x, collapse = "\x01") (non-printable separator). Decode strsplit(key, "\x01", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]. Use R environments (hash = TRUE) O(1) lookup.  [BuildHON KLD division zero]: theory, KLD(, b) hit p_a/0 target exists extended source base source. practice, can’t happen original algorithm every occurrence extended source (,B)→C also counted base source (B)→C, extended passes min_freq, base also passes. Safe return Inf theoretical case. [BuildHON SequenceToNode]: pipe notation reverses history: ("X","","B") → "B|.X" (current state first, history -recent-first, dot-separated). Python pops end remaining seq; R equivalent rev(seq[-length(seq)]).  [pyHON vs pathpy3]: pyHON builds variable-order HON (novel contribution). pathpy3 builds fixed-order HON (specify k). Different algorithms, different outputs. Validate pyHON, pathpy3. [reticulate RETICULATE_PYTHON]: macOS homebrew Python, must set Sys.setenv(RETICULATE_PYTHON = "/opt/homebrew/bin/python3") calling py_available(). Without , reticulate may fail find Python even ’s installed. [BuildHON min_freq threshold bug]: Python’s BuildDistributions zeros Count[Source][Target] place min_freq. KLDThreshold uses sum(Count[ext].values()) includes zeros — total reflects surviving counts. R must also zero count place (just filter computing distributions), otherwise .hon_kld_threshold uses unfiltered totals, producing lower thresholds spurious higher-order rules. Found via group_regulation dataset R 86 edges vs Python’s 78. [reticulate dollar assignment testthat::test_file]: reticulate::py$x <- val inside helper called testthat::test_file() causes “object ‘reticulate’ found” error $<- operator namespace-qualified object requires package attached (just namespace-loaded). Fix: use reticulate::py_set_attr(reticulate::py, "x", val) instead. [reticulate py_to_r test_file]: testthat::test_file() runs tests, iterating Python list object (class python.builtin.list) lapply() fails auto-conversion triggered test environment. Fix: call reticulate::py_to_r() Python list first get proper R list, iterate lapply(). [reticulate Python return types R]: Python tuples returned reticulate converted R environments contexts (indexable [[1]]). Returning Python dicts instead ({'key': val}) gives named R lists reliably accessible via e[["key"]].

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [HON node naming]: Changed pipe notation ("B|.X") readable arrow notation ("X -> -> B"). pipe notation placed current state first reversed history (dot-separated). Arrow notation reads naturally left--right state sequence. .hon_sequence_to_node() now simply paste(seq, collapse = " -> "). [HON edge columns unified]: HON edges now match MOGen/HYPA pattern: path (full sequence), (context/-node), (next state), count, probability. Previously /(node names pipe notation) weight (probability , counts). [HON count recovery]: Raw counts HON edges recovered count environment looking count_env[[source_key]][[next_state]], next_state always last element decoded target tuple (invariant rewiring).

title: Saqrlab Project Learnings
text: [R partial matching n_batch]: n_batch function parameter partially matched n=100 passed via ..., “n” prefix “n_batch”. Fix: place n_batch ... function signature — R allows exact-name matching parameters .... [simulate_data complexity]: Generation-time edge cases (tiny_n, heavy_tailed, heteroscedastic, extreme_imbalance, multicollinear) must checked inside generator via opts$complexity. Post-injection cases (na, outliers, ties, duplicates, constant_col, all_na_col) applied generation .inject_complexity_post() universal across types.  [%||% simulate_data.R]: removing utils.R (moved Nestimate), %||% must defined locally simulate_data.R. Placed bottom file — R closures look %||% package namespace call time (definition time), order within file matter.  [R matrix() fills column--column]: matrix(c(0, 0, 10, 10), nrow=2, ncol=2) puts 0 col 1 10 col 2. Confusion: matrix(c(0, 10, 0, 10), nrow=2, ncol=2) gives columns [0,10] — profiles get identical means. Always verify matrix layout print() writing test assertions.   [.equal relative vs absolute tolerance]: .equal(x, y, tolerance=0.1) uses RELATIVE tolerance (mean absolute diff / mean absolute target), absolute. max absolute diff 0.04 can still fail test target values small. Use max(abs(x - y)) < threshold absolute element-wise comparison. [simulate_fa .nearest_pd suitable Sigma]: .nearest_pd() simulate_data.R normalises diagonals 1 (produces correlation matrix). suitable phi (correlation matrix), never Sigma (covariance matrix). Use raw eigenvalue clamp eig$vectors %*% diag(pmax(eig$values, 1e-6)) %*% t(eig$vectors) covariance matrices. [simulate_fa Cholesky data generation]: L <- chol(Sigma) gives upper Cholesky (t(L) %% L = Sigma). Generate Z %*% L Z n×p iid N(0,1). Cov(X) = t(L) %% L = Sigma. Correct fast. [list(data, params) return pattern]: Returning list(data, params) instead data.frame + attr() makes ground truth accessible consumer (JSON, CLI, Python, JS) without R-specific knowledge. attr() R-internal invisible jsonlite::toJSON().

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: vignette covers bootstrap analysis power analysis TNA studies using Saqrlab. ’ll learn : Run bootstrap simulations assess stability Perform power analysis determine sample sizes Use grid simulations comprehensive evaluation Analyze interpret results

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: Bootstrap helps answer: - stable network estimates? - ’s confidence interval edge weights? - centrality rankings reliable?

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: Power analysis helps answer: - many sequences need reliable results? - sequence length sufficient? - sample size network size interact?

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
code: library(tna)  # Generate some data sequences <- simulate_sequences(   n_sequences = 150,   seq_length = 25,   n_states = 5,   seed = 42 )  # Fit original model original_model <- fit_network_model(sequences, "tna")  # Run bootstrap bootstrap_results <- run_bootstrap_simulation(   original_data = sequences,   n_bootstrap = 100,   model_type = "tna",   sample_size = NULL,  # Use original size   parallel = TRUE,   seed = 42 )

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
code: # Evaluate bootstrap performance evaluation <- run_bootstrap_iteration(   bootstrap_results = bootstrap_results,   original_model = original_model )  # View summary print(evaluation)  # Access specific metrics evaluation$edge_stability evaluation$centrality_stability evaluation$confidence_intervals

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
code: # Define stable transitions to test stable_transitions <- list(   c("Plan", "Monitor"),   c("Monitor", "Evaluate") )  # Bootstrap with stability testing bootstrap_results <- run_bootstrap_simulation(   original_data = sequences,   n_bootstrap = 100,   model_type = "tna",   stable_transitions = stable_transitions,   seed = 42 )

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: comprehensive approach uses parameter grids:
code: # Create parameter grid power_grid <- generate_param_grid(   param_ranges = list(     n_sequences = c(25, 300),     seq_length = c(15, 40),     n_states = c(4, 8)   ),   n = 50,   method = "lhs" )  nrow(power_grid)  # Number of conditions head(power_grid)

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
code: # Run power analysis power_results <- run_grid_simulation(   param_grid = power_grid,   n_runs_per_setting = 50,  # Replications per condition   model_type = "tna",   parallel = TRUE,   seed = 42 )

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
code: # Comprehensive analysis analysis <- summarize_grid_results(power_results)  # Results by sample size analysis$by_n_sequences  # Results by sequence length analysis$by_seq_length  # Results by number of states analysis$by_n_states  # Interaction effects analysis$interactions

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
code: # Find minimum n_sequences for target correlation target_correlation <- 0.90  summary_by_n <- summarize_simulation(   results = power_results,   by = "n_sequences",   metrics = c("mean", "ci_lower") )  # Find first sample size meeting target min_n <- summary_by_n$n_sequences[   which(summary_by_n$correlation_mean >= target_correlation)[1] ]  cat("Minimum sample size for r >=", target_correlation, ":", min_n)

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: specific scenarios, run targeted simulations:
code: # Define ground truth true_probs <- generate_probabilities(n_states = 5, seed = 42)  # Test specific sample sizes sample_sizes <- c(25, 50, 75, 100, 125, 150, 200, 300, 500)  power_results <- lapply(sample_sizes, function(n) {   # Run multiple replications   correlations <- sapply(1:100, function(rep) {     # Generate sequences     seqs <- simulate_sequences(       trans_matrix = true_probs$transition_matrix,       init_probs = true_probs$initial_probs,       n_sequences = n,       seq_length = 25,       seed = rep * 1000 + n     )      # Fit model     model <- fit_network_model(seqs, "tna")      # Compare to truth     estimated <- extract_transition_matrix(model, type = "scaled")     cor(as.vector(true_probs$transition_matrix),         as.vector(estimated))   })    data.frame(     n_sequences = n,     mean_r = mean(correlations),     sd_r = sd(correlations),     ci_lower = mean(correlations) - 1.96 * sd(correlations),     ci_upper = mean(correlations) + 1.96 * sd(correlations),     prop_above_90 = mean(correlations >= 0.90)   ) })  power_df <- do.call(rbind, power_results) print(power_df)

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(power_df, aes(x = n_sequences, y = mean_r)) + geom_line() +   geom_point() +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), alpha = 0.2) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.90, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +   labs(     x = "Number of Sequences",     y = "Mean Correlation with True Network",     title = "Power Analysis: Network Recovery by Sample Size"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: Beyond correlation, examine edge recovery:
code: # For each sample size, calculate edge recovery recovery_results <- lapply(sample_sizes, function(n) {   recoveries <- lapply(1:50, function(rep) {     seqs <- simulate_sequences(       trans_matrix = true_probs$transition_matrix,       init_probs = true_probs$initial_probs,       n_sequences = n,       seq_length = 25,       seed = rep * 1000 + n     )      model <- fit_network_model(seqs, "tna")      # Create pseudo-model from true matrix     true_model <- list(weights = true_probs$transition_matrix)      recovery <- compare_edge_recovery(       original = true_model,       simulated = model,       threshold = 0.05     )      c(precision = recovery$precision,       recall = recovery$recall,       f1 = recovery$f1_score)   })    df <- do.call(rbind, recoveries)   data.frame(     n_sequences = n,     precision_mean = mean(df[,"precision"]),     recall_mean = mean(df[,"recall"]),     f1_mean = mean(df[,"f1"])   ) })  recovery_df <- do.call(rbind, recovery_results) print(recovery_df)

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: Typically important factor:
code: # Typical power curve behavior # - Steep improvement from 25-100 # - Diminishing returns after 200-300 # - Asymptotic approach to perfect recovery

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: Longer sequences provide transitions:
code: # Compare different sequence lengths length_grid <- generate_param_grid(   param_ranges = list(     n_sequences = c(50, 200),     seq_length = c(10, 50),     n_states = c(5, 5)   ),   n = 30,   method = "lhs" )  length_results <- run_grid_simulation(   param_grid = length_grid,   n_runs_per_setting = 30,   model_type = "tna",   seed = 42 )

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: states = parameters estimate:
code: # Compare different network sizes states_grid <- generate_param_grid(   param_ranges = list(     n_sequences = c(50, 300),     seq_length = c(25, 25),     n_states = c(3, 10)   ),   n = 30,   method = "lhs" )  states_results <- run_grid_simulation(   param_grid = states_grid,   n_runs_per_setting = 30,   model_type = "tna",   seed = 42 )

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: Sparser networks may harder recover:
code: # Test different network densities # Use generate_probabilities with different parameters sparse_net <- generate_probabilities(n_states = 6, edge_density = 0.3) dense_net <- generate_probabilities(n_states = 6, edge_density = 0.8)

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
code: # 1. Define parameter space full_grid <- generate_param_grid(   param_ranges = list(     n_sequences = c(25, 500),     seq_length = c(15, 40),     n_states = c(4, 8)   ),   n = 100,   method = "lhs" )  # 2. Run comprehensive simulation full_results <- run_grid_simulation(   param_grid = full_grid,   n_runs_per_setting = 100,   model_type = "tna",   parallel = TRUE,   cores = parallel::detectCores() - 1,   seed = 42,   verbose = TRUE )  # 3. Analyze results analysis <- summarize_grid_results(full_results)  # 4. Generate power tables power_table <- summarize_simulation(   full_results,   by = c("n_sequences", "seq_length", "n_states"),   metrics = c("mean", "sd", "ci_lower", "ci_upper") )  # 5. Find recommended sample sizes recommendations <- power_table[power_table$correlation_ci_lower >= 0.85, ] min_by_condition <- aggregate(   n_sequences ~ seq_length + n_states,   data = recommendations,   FUN = min ) print(min_by_condition)

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
code: # Generate summary statistics summary_stats <- summarize_simulation(   power_results,   by = "n_sequences",   metrics = "all" )  # Create report cat(" Power Analysis Results =====================  Study Parameters: - Sequence length: 25 - Number of states: 5 - Replications per condition: 100 - Target correlation: 0.90  Results by Sample Size: ") print(summary_stats)  cat(" Recommendations: - Minimum sample size for r >= 0.90:", min_n, "sequences - Recommended sample size (with margin):", ceiling(min_n * 1.2), "sequences ")

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: Define research question: parameters affect recovery? Create parameter grid: Use generate_param_grid() Run simulations: Use run_grid_simulation() many replications Analyze results: Use summarize_grid_results() summarize_simulation() Determine minimum sample: Find smallest n meeting target Add safety margin: Recommend 10-20% minimum

title: Bootstrap and Power Analysis
text: Small networks (3-4 states): ~50-100 sequences often sufficient Medium networks (5-7 states): ~100-200 sequences recommended Large networks (8+ states): ~200-500 sequences may needed Sequence length: 20-30 actions typically adequate Always validate: Run pilot simulations specific scenario

title: Hierarchical and Multilevel Network Analysis
text: vignette covers hierarchical (HTNA) multilevel (MLNA) network analysis using Saqrlab. approaches extend standard TNA organizing nodes types levels: HTNA (Hierarchical TNA): Nodes grouped distinct types/categories MLNA (Multilevel TNA): Nodes organized hierarchical levels MTNA (Multi-Type TNA): General term networks typed nodes Saqrlab provides simulate_htna(), simulate_mlna(), simulate_mtna() aliases produce identical output.

title: Hierarchical and Multilevel Network Analysis
text: standard TNA, nodes treated equally. HTNA/MLNA: Nodes types: node belongs category Within-type patterns: Transitions within type -type patterns: Transitions across different types Structured visualization: Layout reflects node groupings

title: Hierarchical and Multilevel Network Analysis
text: Multi-type networks useful studying: Self-regulated learning: Metacognitive, cognitive, behavioral dimensions Collaborative learning: Individual vs. group regulation Multimodal data: Different data sources (logs, surveys, observations) Hierarchical processes: Macro, meso, micro levels analysis

title: Hierarchical and Multilevel Network Analysis
code: # Default: 5 types x 5 nodes = 25-node network net <- simulate_htna(seed = 42)  # Network dimensions dim(net$matrix) #> [1] 25 25  # Node types names(net$node_types) #> [1] "Metacognitive" "Cognitive"     "Behavioral"    "Social"        #> [5] "Motivational"  # Nodes per type net$n_nodes_per_type #> Metacognitive     Cognitive    Behavioral        Social  Motivational  #>             5             5             5             5             5

title: Hierarchical and Multilevel Network Analysis
code: # High within-type, low between-type connectivity clustered <- simulate_htna(   n_nodes = 4,   n_types = 3,   within_prob = 0.6,    # 60% within-type edges   between_prob = 0.1,   # 10% between-type edges   seed = 42 )  # More integrated network integrated <- simulate_htna(   n_nodes = 4,   n_types = 3,   within_prob = 0.3,    # 30% within-type   between_prob = 0.3,   # 30% between-type (equal)   seed = 42 )

title: Hierarchical and Multilevel Network Analysis
code: # Function to analyze within/between type edge counts analyze_connectivity <- function(net) {   mat <- net$matrix   n_types <- length(net$node_types)   n_per_type <- net$n_nodes_per_type[1]    within_count <- 0   between_count <- 0    for (i in 1:nrow(mat)) {     for (j in 1:ncol(mat)) {       type_i <- ceiling(i / n_per_type)       type_j <- ceiling(j / n_per_type)        if (mat[i, j] > 0) {         if (type_i == type_j) {           within_count <- within_count + 1         } else {           between_count <- between_count + 1         }       }     }   }    list(     within = within_count,     between = between_count,     ratio = within_count / (within_count + between_count)   ) }  # Compare networks analyze_connectivity(clustered) #> $within #> [1] 21 #>  #> $between #> [1] 8 #>  #> $ratio #> [1] 0.7241379 analyze_connectivity(integrated) #> $within #> [1] 9 #>  #> $between #> [1] 23 #>  #> $ratio #> [1] 0.28125

title: Hierarchical and Multilevel Network Analysis
code: library(tna)  net <- simulate_htna(seed = 42)  # Polygon layout (types arranged in circle) plot_htna(   weights = net$matrix,   node_groups = net$node_types,   layout = "polygon" )

code: # Layer layout (types as horizontal layers) plot_mlna(   weights = net$matrix,   layers = net$node_types )

title: Hierarchical and Multilevel Network Analysis
code: # Generate sequences from HTNA matrix net <- simulate_htna(seed = 42)  # Create sequences using the transition matrix sequences <- simulate_sequences(   trans_matrix = net$matrix,   init_probs = rep(1/nrow(net$matrix), nrow(net$matrix)),   n_sequences = 200,   seq_length = 30 )  # Fit TNA model model <- fit_network_model(sequences, "tna")  # The model can be visualized with type information plot_htna(   weights = extract_transition_matrix(model),   node_groups = net$node_types )

title: Hierarchical and Multilevel Network Analysis
code: # Extract submatrices for each type pair extract_type_block <- function(net, type1, type2) {   nodes1 <- net$node_types[[type1]]   nodes2 <- net$node_types[[type2]]   net$matrix[nodes1, nodes2] }  net <- simulate_htna(seed = 42)  # Within Metacognitive meta_block <- extract_type_block(net, "Metacognitive", "Metacognitive")  # Metacognitive to Cognitive meta_to_cog <- extract_type_block(net, "Metacognitive", "Cognitive")
